name: MCPProbe
description: The definitive diagnostic layer for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) ecosystem. Instantly probe any MCP server to extract tools, verify client compatibility, and generate production configs.

sections.title: Introspective Server Analysis
sections.content: Engineered a robust scanning engine that establishes Stdio or SSE connections to perform full introspective analysis. It extracts tool schemas, resource templates, and prompt definitions, providing a clear map of server capabilities.

sections.title: Client Compatibility Matrix
sections.content: Developed a rigid verification layer that tests server implementations against specific requirements of Cursor, Claude Desktop, and VS Code. This ensures developers can ship MCP tools that work reliably across the entire AI interface landscape.

sections.title: Smart Config Orchestration
sections.content: Implemented an automated generator that outputs production-ready JSON configurations tailored to individual AI clients. It handles path resolution, environment variables, and client-specific boilerplate with zero manual intervention.

learnings: The MCP ecosystem lacks standardized validation; a central diagnostic tool is essential for developer trust and rapid prototyping.
Asynchronous transport discovery (Stdio vs SSE) is the most fragile part of the protocol—robust retry logic and timeouts are mandatory.
Quantitative 'Health Scores' significantly improve developer focus by gamifying the iterative improvement of tool documentation.

name: Autonoma
description: Professional-grade AI Agent platform that operates as a 'Digital FTE'. Features proactive monitoring, high-fidelity live telemetry HUD, and intelligent multi-session memory across Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord, and Gmail.

sections.title: Proactive HUD & Telemetry
sections.content: Engineered a high-fidelity 'Control Tower' using React 19. The dashboard provides live Gantt-style visualizations of agent reasoning loops, pipeline latency tracking, and proactive system health alerts through a premium glassmorphic interface.

sections.title: Neural Memory Registry
sections.content: Implemented a sophisticated memory engine using SQLite with FTS5. It employs BM25 ranked retrieval, importance-based decay, and automatic deduplication to ensure the agent maintains high-signal context across multi-session interactions.

sections.title: Unified Channel Gateway
sections.content: Developed a robust integration layer supporting mixed-media communication. The gateway handles complex routing between WebSockets, Telegram, Discord, and a Node.js-based WhatsApp bridge, providing a singular, abstracted API for the agent cortex.

learnings: Proactive monitoring (push) significantly improves the perceived 'agency' of AI systems compared to purely reactive models.
BM25 ranking for local memory outperforms simple vector search for technical task retrieval where keyword precision is critical.
Developing a 'Soul Evolution' system with identity diff-tracking is essential for auditability in autonomous digital employees.

name: FerrumDB
description: High-performance, embedded document database engine written from scratch in Rust. Features O(1) performance, AES-256 encryption, and atomic transactions.

sections.title: Bitcask-Inspired AOF
sections.content: Engineered a custom binary log format using an append-only file (AOF) architecture. This design optimizes for high-throughput sequential I/O, ensuring deterministic recovery and crash-resilience through deterministic log replay.

sections.title: Pluggable I/O Abstraction
sections.content: Implemented a sophisticated AsyncFileSystem trait that abstracts disk operations. This allowed for a decorator-based encryption layer, seamlessly wrapping standard I/O with AES-256-GCM authentication tags for each data block.

sections.title: Ferrum Studio
sections.content: Developed a built-in observability dashboard using Axum and lock-free atomic metrics. It provides real-time telemetry on operations per second, lookup counts, and database health metrics at sub-microsecond precision.

learnings: Rust's ownership model is uniquely suited for building lock-free storage engines with high concurrency.
The decorator pattern is powerful for layering cross-cutting concerns like encryption and logging without polluting core logic.
Embedded databases must prioritize memory efficiency; O(1) reads come at the cost of ram-resident indexes.

name: CodeLens
description: The Neural Bridge Between Developer & Codebase. A high-fidelity architectural autopilot synthesizing massive repositories into an interactive neural index.

sections.title: Neural RAG Pipeline
sections.content: Engineered a sophisticated Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline grounded in source code truth. It transcends simple keyword matching by understanding developer intent through high-dimensional vector synthesis and semantic contextualization.

sections.title: Architectural Discovery
sections.content: A glassmorphic, high-focus interface designed to map out structural integrity and logic flow. From onboarding new developers to auditing complex legacy systems, CodeLens provides the strategic depth required for modern engineering.

sections.title: Incremental SHA-256 Indexing
sections.content: Implemented hash-based delta analysis for codebase ingestion. By tracking file changes with SHA-256, only modified fragments are re-embedded, drastically reducing latency and operational costs for private repository analysis.

learnings: Sub-millisecond vector retrieval is the primary bottleneck for building truly conversational architectural interfaces.
AST-aware chunking outperforms standard character-based splitting by preserving the semantic integrity of classes and functions.
Deploying Next.js 16/React 19 applications requires a 'Server-First' mindset for optimal heavy-lifting AI tasks.

name: Go Load Balancer
description: Production-grade HTTP load balancer with advanced observability. Features multi-strategy routing, distributed tracing, and zero-downtime hot reloads.

sections.title: Advanced Traffic Orchestration
sections.content: Implemented five distinct load-balancing strategies: Round-Robin, Least Connections, Weighted RR, IP Hash, and Random with Two Choices. This allows for granular control over traffic distribution based on backend capacity and persistence requirements.

sections.title: Circuit Breaker & Resilience
sections.content: Engineered a state-aware circuit breaker pattern (Closed, Half-Open, Open) with configurable failure thresholds. By stopping traffic to failing nodes, the system prevents cascading failures and provides deterministic recovery windows.

sections.title: Full-Stack Observability
sections.content: Integrated a comprehensive observability suite featuring Prometheus metrics for P99 latency tracking and OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing. Every request is injected with a unique X-Request-ID for end-to-end auditability.

learnings: Hot-reload configuration using file-watchers is key for minimizing operational friction in high-availability systems.
Passive health checking based on actual proxy error rates detects degradation much faster than periodic active probes alone.
Effective load balancing isn't just about routing; it's about connection pooling and intelligent request retry management.

name: Echo AI Agent
description: The Ultimate JARVIS-Inspired Neural Interface. A holographic, liquid-morphic AI agent featuring real-time particle physics, multi-model reasoning, and a privacy-first STT/TTS engine.

sections.title: Holographic Liquid-Morphic UI
sections.content: Engineered a state-of-the-art visual engine using Canvas and complex particle systems. The HUD features 2,000+ particles that react dynamically to voice frequency bands, creating a liquid-morphic 'Emerald' interface inspired by deep sci-fi aesthetics.

sections.title: Hybrid Voice Intelligence
sections.content: Implemented a multi-tier Speech-to-Text (STT) layer supporting Browser APIs, Whisper Cloud, and Local Whisper binaries for 100% private execution. Combined with Neural TTS profiles, Echo provides a zero-latency conversational loop.

sections.title: System-Level Function Calling
sections.content: Developed a robust orchestration layer that enables Echo to execute shell commands, manage system files, and interact with third-party APIs through secure function calling. Includes a local, encrypted memory vault for context persistence.

learnings: Visual fidelity is a functional requirement for AI agents—a high-quality interface creates the 'personality' that drives user adoption.
VAD (Voice Activity Detection) is the hardest part of the conversational loop; balancing accuracy with sub-100ms response times is critical.
Local-first AI agents require strict resource management, especially when running concurrent particle simulations and neural inference.

name: ELYX
description: Local-First Autonomous AI Employee. A multi-platform orchestration engine that monitors email, social media, and ERPs to execute business logic via HITL workflows.

sections.title: Perception Layer
sections.content: Engineered a Python-based watching system using Playwright and standard APIs to monitor disjointed communication channels. The layer synthesizes raw platform events into structured Markdown tasks for the reasoning engine.

sections.title: Obsidian-as-Memory
sections.content: Pioneered a 'Local-First' memory architecture using an Obsidian vault. By treating Markdown files as state-machines, ELYX provides deep transparency—every plan, log, and approval cycle is an immutable technical artifact.

sections.title: HITL Approval Cycles
sections.content: Implemented a rigid Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) protocol for sensitive operations. The orchestrator generates execution plans in a 'Pending' state, requiring the user to physically move files in the vault to authorize high-risk actions.

learnings: Autonomous agents are only as reliable as their 'perception' fidelity; handling platform-specific edge cases is the hardest part.
Markdown is a surprisingly robust state management tool for local-first AI systems due to its human-readable nature.
A truly swappable 'Brain Factory' requires strict output normalization to handle different LLM reasoning styles.

name: Promptly
description: Smart Context-Aware Prompt Engineering. An MCP server that bridges the gap between AI intent and codebase reality through automated analysis and refinement.

sections.title: Codebase Context Injection
sections.content: Engineered a high-speed analyzer that maps project structure, naming conventions, and dependency trees. By injecting this 'ground truth' directly into the prompt via the Model Context Protocol, the tool drastically reduces AI hallucinations and logic errors.

sections.title: Agent-Specific Refinement
sections.content: Implemented a sophisticated rules-engine that tunes prompts for specific AI agents (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor). It contextually applies success criteria, imperative constraints, and file relevance scoring based on the detected tech stack.

sections.title: Zero-Friction Orchestration
sections.content: Designed an automated setup wizard that configures global or project-level MCP settings. The architecture features intelligent caching with automatic invalidation on manifest changes (package.json, tsconfig.json), ensuring minimal overhead.

learnings: The 'Ground Truth' gap is the primary reason for AI coding failures; bridging it with local context is more effective than larger context windows.
Developer tools must have sub-100ms latency to feel invisible—caching and incremental analysis are mandatory for high-velocity workflows.
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is evolving into the standard 'nerve system' for AI-to-IDE communication.

name: Physical AI Platform
description: Next-generation Embodied Intelligence textbook. Features a RAG-powered AI tutor, personalized learning paths for ROS 2, and real-time Urdu translation for robotics education.

sections.title: Context-Aware AI Tutor
sections.content: Engineered a sophisticated RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system using Qdrant and Gemini 2.0. The TA is grounded specifically in the textbook's technical content, providing sub-second answers to complex ROS 2 and kinematics queries while referencing internal citations.

sections.title: Embodied Intelligence Curriculum
sections.content: Architected a comprehensive 13-week technical journey from basic ROS 2 nodes to advanced VLA (Vision-Language-Action) models. The platform integrates NVIDIA Isaac Sim for photorealistic robotics simulation and digital-twin training.

sections.title: Dynamic Personalization Engine
sections.content: Implemented a multi-tier onboarding system using Better Auth and Neon Postgres. The textbook dynamically adjust its technical depth (Beginner → Advanced) based on the user's hardware and software background, ensuring an optimal learning curve.

learnings: Physical AI requires a fundamental bridge between digital reasoning (LLMs) and temporal/physical constraints of real-world hardware.
Vector-based RAG for technical documentation must prioritize code-snippet preservation to remain useful for engineering education.
Hybrid backends (FastAPI for AI + Node.js for Auth) provide the best balance of speed and security for AI-native platforms.

name: Notion Autopilot
description: Autonomous Workspace Intelligence & Organization. An AI agent that continuously crawls, organizes, and extracts cognitive insights from messy Notion environments.

sections.title: Autonomous Workspace Crawler
sections.content: Engineered a resilient Notion API client capable of full-workspace discovery. The agent crawls pages, databases, and blocks to identify 'Chaos Scores'—orphan pages, dead links, and duplicate clusters—before executing autonomous cleanup protocols.

sections.title: Cognitive Pattern Recognition
sections.content: Developed a reasoning engine that synthesizes raw workspace content into a 'Brain Briefing'. By analyzing recurring themes and contradictions across disjointed notes, the agent surfaces meta-insights about the user's focus areas and blind spots.

sections.title: Multi-Provider Orchestration
sections.content: Built a provider-agnostic LLM interface supporting low-latency local execution (Ollama) and high-speed cloud inference (Groq/Gemini). This architecture allows for cost-effective, bulk categorization of thousands of Notion blocks without vendor lock-in.

learnings: The hardest part of autonomous organization isn't the AI—it's building a reliable, state-aware API crawler that respects platform limits.
Machine-generated 'meta-insights' are far more valuable to a user than simple auto-tagging of tasks.
Providing a full 'organizer' history is critical for building trust in autonomous systems that modify user-owned data.

name: OpsForge
description: Zero-dependency DevOps CLI & REST API toolkit built with Go's standard library. Features 10+ core utilities for load testing, SSL auditing, and network discovery.

sections.title: Zero-Dependency Architecture
sections.content: Engineered a production-ready toolkit using exclusively the Go standard library. By avoiding third-party dependencies, the project achieves an ultra-low attack surface and maximum long-term maintainability for mission-critical DevOps environments.

sections.title: Concurrent Task Orchestration
sections.content: Leverages bounded concurrency models with goroutines and semaphores. The port-scanner and load-tester modules utilize a fixed worker pool to manage thousands of parallel TCP/HTTP connections without exhausting system file descriptors.

sections.title: API-First Extensibility
sections.content: Features a built-in 'devtool serve' mode that exposes all CLI capabilities as a REST API. Includes native Prometheus metrics exposition and a plugin discovery system that automatically registers external binaries from the system PATH.

learnings: Building without external frameworks like Cobra forces a deeper understanding of Go's flag parsing and command-routing primitives.
Structured logging with log/slog is essential for creating machine-readable CLI outputs suitable for modern CI/CD pipelines.
Multi-stage Docker builds using the 'scratch' image provide the most secure and efficient way to distribute compiled binaries.

name: Automation Vault
description: Enterprise-Grade Automation Infrastructure & AI Orchestration. A collection of complex, event-driven workflows that automate the entire Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC).

sections.title: Full-Stack Project Monitor
sections.content: Engineered a massive, 30KB+ n8n workflow that serves as a self-healing project monitor. It intercepts GitHub webhooks to perform real-time AI code reviews, detect security vulnerabilities, and auto-generate executive briefings for project stakeholders.

sections.title: Autonomous Documentation Pipeline
sections.content: Implemented a recursive documentation engine that scans README files for gaps and automatically generates missing technical sections. The system creates automated PRs and synchronizes updates with internal wikis and Google Docs simultaneously.

sections.title: AI Lead Qualification
sections.content: Architected a multi-step nurture system that leverages Groq for ultra-high-speed lead qualification. The system synthesizes incoming data points into structured buyer personas, triggering personalized email sequences based on behavioral triggers.

sections.title: Integrated Workflow Library
sections.content:
The repository serves as a centralized hub for production-ready automation protocols, including:

• Full-Stack Project Monitor & AI Reviewer
• Autonomous AI Support Infrastructure
• Multi-Channel Content Distribution Hub
• Real Estate Lead Nurture Ecosystem
• LinkedIn AI Post & Lead Automation
• Abandoned Cart Recovery Protocol
• Neural Intelligence Newsletter Engine

learnings: Low-code doesn't equal low-complexity; enterprise automation requires rigorous error-handling, retry logic, and architectural discipline.
The value of automation isn't just time saved—it's the elimination of cognitive overhead for engineering teams.
Event-driven architectures are the only way to build truly responsive business and technical infrastructure.

name: THE SIGNAL
description: Premium AI Intelligence Protocol. A fully automated engine synthesizing global AI news into high-fidelity briefings via a strict 3-3-2-2-1 data protocol.

sections.title: Intelligence Synthesis
sections.content: Engineered a weekly intelligence pipeline that crawls GitHub trending repos and global tech news via NewsAPI. Using Gemini 2.0, the system distills raw data into a premium 3-3-2-2-1 briefing, focusing on high-signal dev tools and AI hardware breakthroughs.

sections.title: Automated Dispatch Workflow
sections.content: Leveraging Inngest's event-driven architecture and Vercel Cron, the engine handles asynchronous task processing with resilient retry logic. It ensures zero-downtime delivery across thousands of global timezones simultaneously.

sections.title: Neural Gamification
sections.content: Implemented a 10-tier loyalty spectrum that evolves the user's interface from 'Alpha Initiate' to 'Signal Architect'. As users reach higher tiers, the entire UI theme dynamically shifts, unlocking total 'Prism Protocol' customization.

learnings: Dynamic timezone delivery requires careful timestamp normalization at the edge to prevent race conditions during global dispatch.
Gamifying a newsletter increases long-term retention by turning consumption into a progression-based technical journey.
Serverless automation via GitHub Actions provides the most cost-effective and reliable orchestrator for consistent weekly content generation.

name: todo-audit
description: High-Performance Static Technical Debt Auditor. A Rust-based CLI engine that scans massive codebases to track, audit, and visualize technical debt with lazy Git-blame integration.

sections.title: Static Technical Debt Tracking
sections.content: Engineered a recursive scanning engine that accurately captures TODO, FIXME, and HACK annotations across any codebase. The tool respects .gitignore automatically by leveraging the high-performance 'ignore' crate architecture.

sections.title: Lazy Git-Blame Orchestration
sections.content: Implemented a lazy evaluation strategy for Git metadata. Blame data (author, staleness, timestamp) is only fetched when specific filters are triggered, ensuring the core scanning performance remains ultra-fast for large repositories.

sections.title: CI/CD Gate Integration
sections.content: Designed with a Unix-first philosophy, allowing todo-audit to be integrated into CI pipelines as a quality gate. Supports multiple output formats (Text, JSON) for automated reporting and debt orchestration.

learnings: Effective static analysis tools must prioritize performance; if a scan takes more than a few seconds, it won't be used.
Git-blame porcelain output is powerful but expensive; batching and lazy loading are mandatory for enterprise-scale repositories.
Developer experience (DX) is the primary feature of CLI tools—clear error codes and intuitive flags are non-negotiable.

name: VidX
description: High-Fidelity Video Transformation for the Web. An interactive CLI tool that abstracts complex FFmpeg operations into a guided TUI for professional web optimization.

sections.title: FFmpeg Abstraction Layer
sections.content: Engineered a non-leaky abstraction over FFmpeg's low-level flags. By synthesizing complex parameters into 'Web Optimized' and 'High Quality' presets, VidX allows developers to achieve perfect web-ready encodes without mastering the libx264/libvpx-vp9 syntax.

sections.title: GIF-to-Video Transformation
sections.content: Implemented an automated protocol for converting aging .gif assets into high-performance, looping MP4/WebM videos. This includes automatic audio-stripping (-an) and specific flags for web-streaming (+faststart), resulting in 90%+ reductions in asset size.

sections.title: Universal Runtime Compatibility
sections.content: Designed a robust detection engine that bridges system-level FFmpeg binaries with bundled fallbacks. This 'zero-manual-setup' architecture ensures consistent performance across macOS, Windows, and Linux environments without the need for manual path configuration.

learnings: The primary hurdle for web video adoption is CLI complexity—reducing this friction directly improves the performance of the modern web.
AV1 is the future of web video, but standard library fallbacks are still mandatory for comprehensive cross-browser compatibility.
TUI (Terminal User Interfaces) provide a perfect middle ground between rigid CLI flags and heavy GUI applications for engineering workflows.
